title: Interesting Article on NGE
wikitext:
[]: Interesting Article on NGE...             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 25, 2007 9:34 pm Found this a while ago, but I just got around to reading it: NGE Article a lot of it concerns the state of anime at the time NGE came around, and its historical signifcance. It also focuses a lot on Anno's inverting and subverting of his narrative devices. I love the end where he compares NGE's 2nd half as "Godard like". To those who've seen his works, I think that comparison is a really astute and accurate one. This is probably old, but I was wondering what others thought of it...

[]: NGE article             Quote Post by Thanatos » Tue Jul 03, 2007 12:44 am Well thats was a very interesting read, oddly enough i read an article by the very same author. Hiroki Azuma is a quite the character. He publishes papers covering a wide variety of topics in Japanese pop culture and general philosphy. This is his website with some of his published works being viewable in English. The article you linked it the first one:Anime or Something like It. Check out article VI Superflat Japanese Postmodernism. [url]http://www.hirokiazuma.com/en/index.html[/url] Its an exhausting read, but very insightful look into otaku culture. It isn't primarily about NGE as his other article was, but it is referenced throughout.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jul 03, 2007 12:56 am Thanks for the link Thanatos because I couldn't find anything else about him or where that article came from. I thought it was really fascinating too. His summation in his comparing NGE to Godard jumped out at me because it's a comparison that didn't even occur to me until he mentioned it, but the more I've thought about it, the more I see the connection.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 03, 2007 1:02 am I hate to seem completely arrogant here, but who is the GODARD this article refers to? Jean-Luc Godard? Otherwise, very good article, except for the occasional punctuational error.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jul 03, 2007 1:20 am NAveryW wrote: I hate to seem completely arrogant here, but who is the GODARD this article refers to? Jean-Luc Godard?

It also focuses a lot on Anno's inverting and subverting of his narrative devices. I love the end where he compares NGE's 2nd half as "Godard like". To those who've seen his works, I think that comparison is a really astute and accurate one. This is probably old, but I was wondering what others thought of it...

Check out article VI Superflat Japanese Postmodernism. [url]http://www.hirokiazuma.com/en/index.html[/url] Its an exhausting read, but very insightful look into otaku culture. It isn't primarily about NGE as his other article was, but it is referenced throughout.

His summation in his comparing NGE to Godard jumped out at me because it's a comparison that didn't even occur to me until he mentioned it, but the more I've thought about it, the more I see the connection.
